user_query:
I keep local Markdown guides for specific chess openings and I regularly download PGN games from my online play. Please set up a small, file-based, event-triggered automation that watches the inbox/ folder for new .pgn files and updates my opening guides and a summary report. Use the provided sample files to verify your implementation and make it idempotent.

Requirements:
1) Watcher:
- Create a lightweight cross-platform watcher (e.g., a polling script) that scans inbox/ for new .pgn files every few seconds and uses a ledger at state/processed.json to skip files already handled. Do not rely on external services or the Internet.
- The watcher should trigger a processing run when it sees at least one unprocessed .pgn file. Re-running the watcher should not duplicate entries or corrupt files.

2) Tracked openings:
- The set of tracked openings is defined by the H1 heading in each Markdown guide under notes/. In the provided input, these are notes/Sicilian_Defense.md ("# Sicilian Defense") and notes/Queens_Gambit_Declined.md ("# Queen's Gambit Declined").
- When parsing PGNs, map the [Opening] tag to a base opening name by taking the substring before the first colon (":"), trimming whitespace. Only process games whose base opening matches one of the tracked openings exactly.

3) Processing on new PGNs:
- Inspect files and directories to find all notes/*.md guides and all unprocessed .pgn files in inbox/.
- Parse PGN tags: Opening, ECO, Date, White, Black, Result, WhiteElo, BlackElo. You may ignore moves; rely on tags.
- Filter to tracked openings only. For each tracked opening encountered, compute per-opening aggregates using all archived and newly processed PGNs: games, white_wins, black_wins, draws, avg_elo (average over games of (WhiteElo+BlackElo)/2, rounded sensibly), and eco_codes_present (unique ECO codes seen for that opening).
- Sort/rank: Prepare a CSV at reports/opening_summary.csv with header: opening,eco_codes_present,games,white_wins,black_wins,draws,avg_elo. Include only tracked openings, sorted by games descending, then by opening ascending. Overwrite the CSV on each run.
- Update guides: For each affected notes/<Opening>.md, replace the lines between the markers "<!-- RECENT_GAMES:START -->" and "<!-- RECENT_GAMES:END -->" with up to the three highest-average-Elo games for that opening across all processed PGNs (archived + new), sorted by avg Elo descending and using most recent Date to break ties. Each line should include: YYYY-MM-DD, White vs Black, Result, ECO, and the relative path to the archived PGN. Edit the existing local documents in place.
- After processing, move each newly handled .pgn from inbox/ to archive/ (preserve the original filename), and record its archive path in state/processed.json so it won’t be reprocessed.

Deliverables and paths:
- scripts/ (your watcher/processor code)
- reports/opening_summary.csv (regenerated on each run as described)
- notes/Sicilian_Defense.md and notes/Queens_Gambit_Declined.md updated between the markers
- archive/ with the processed .pgn files moved from inbox/
- state/processed.json updated to include the archived file paths

Use only the local files provided. Do not download anything. The automation should be verifiable using the included sample PGNs in inbox/ and the initial notes/ content. Please include brief instructions in a comment at the top of your script on how to start/stop the watcher. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
